- `Models/ChatMessage.cs` вҖ” individual messages within a session
- `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` вҖ” EF Core DbContext for Postgres

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `Services/DocumentIngestionService.cs`
  - Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
  - Extract text using PdfPig
  - Split into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)
  - Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
  - Store chunks + embeddings in Postgres/pgvector
  - Skip already-ingested files (hash-based dedup)

### 4. Chat service (RAG pipeline)
- `Services/ChatService.cs`
  - Accept user query
  - Generate query embedding
  - Vector similarity search against document chunks (top-5, min score 0.75)
  - Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution
  - Stream LLM response via `IChatClient`
  - Persist messages to chat history

### 5. Blazor UI
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` вҖ” app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` вҖ” main chat interface
  - Message history display
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming response rendering
  - Source document references shown per response
- `wwwroot/css/app.css` вҖ” chat-specific styling

### 6. Configuration & startup
- Wire up DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, DbContext, services
- Add health checks for Postgres and OpenAI
- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
- EF Core migrations for database schema

### 7. Guardrails (from technology-selection)
- Set temperature=0 for factual Q&A
- Set MaxOutputTokens to control cost
- Pin model version (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18)
- Implement retry logic on LLM calls
- Set relevance threshold on vector search (0.75)
- Source attribution in responses
- API key from environment/user-secrets, never hardcoded
- Log token usage per request

## Notes & Considerations

- **pgvector extension**: Postgres must have the `vector` extension installed. The app will attempt `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector` during migration.
- **Chunking strategy**: Paragraph-based semantic chunking with ~500 token windows and 50-token overlap for context continuity.
- **Embedding dimensions**: `text-embedding-3-small` produces 1536-dimensional vectors.
- **Streaming**: Use `GetStreamingResponseAsync` + `IAsyncEnumerable` for time-to-first-token optimization in the chat UI.
- **File watching**: Initial version ingests on startup. Future enhancement: `FileSystemWatcher` for hot-reload when new PDFs are added.
- **No authentication**: Initial version is internal-only, no auth. Can be added later.

| `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | EF Core provider for Postgres |
| `pgvector` (Npgsql plugin) | pgvector support for embeddings |
| `UglyToad.PdfPig` | PDF text extraction |

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all required NuGet packages
- Add Blazor Server services and configuration to `Program.cs`
- Create `appsettings.json` with configuration sections (AI, ConnectionStrings)

### 2. Data models & EF Core context
- `Models/DocumentChunk.cs` вҖ” represents a chunk of PDF content with its embedding vector
- `Models/ChatSession.cs` вҖ” a user chat session
- `Models/ChatMessage.cs` вҖ” individual messages within a session
- `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` вҖ” EF Core DbContext for Postgres

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `Services/DocumentIngestionService.cs`
  - Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
  - Extract text using PdfPig
  - Split into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)
  - Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
  - Store chunks + embeddings in Postgres/pgvector
  - Skip already-ingested files (hash-based dedup)

### 4. Chat service (RAG pipeline)
- `Services/ChatService.cs`
  - Accept user query
  - Generate query embedding
  - Vector similarity search against document chunks (top-5, min score 0.75)
  - Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution
  - Stream LLM response via `IChatClient`
  - Persist messages to chat history

- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` вҖ” app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` вҖ” main chat interface
  - Message history display
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming response rendering
  - Source document references shown per response

- Wire up DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, DbContext, services
- Add health checks for Postgres and OpenAI
- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
- EF Core migrations for database schema

- Set temperature=0 for factual Q&A
- Set MaxOutputTokens to control cost
- Pin model version (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18)

- Set relevance threshold on vector search (0.75)

# HelpChatbot вҖ” RAG-based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem

Build a web-based LLM chatbot using Blazor that helps customers get answers from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Technology Selection

Based on the .NET AI technology decision tree, this is a **RAG pipeline** (semantic search + LLM generation over unstructured text). The correct stack is:

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **UI** | Blazor Server (.NET 10) | Interactive chat UI with server-side rendering, real-time streaming |
| **LLM abstraction** | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic chat interface; single promptвҶ’response with context injection (no agentic loops needed) |
| **LLM provider** | OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini) | Cost-effective for Q&A; swap later via MEAI abstraction |
| **Embeddings** | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` via `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Generates vectors for chunks and queries |
| **Vector store** | Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData + Npgsql/pgvector provider | Postgres-native vector similarity search |
| **Document ingestion** | PdfPig for PDF parsing + custom chunking | Extract text from PDFs, split into semantic chunks |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL with pgvector extension | Relational data (chat history) + vector search in one database |
| **ORM** | Entity Framework Core + Npgsql | Chat history, session management |

**Why not Microsoft.Agents.AI?** This is a straightforward RAG scenario (retrieve context вҶ’ augment prompt вҶ’ generate response). No tool calling, multi-step reasoning, or agentic loops are required. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.

## NuGet Packages

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` | IChatClient / IEmbeddingGenerator abstractions |
| `OpenAI` | LLM provider (wires into MEAI) |
| `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` | Vector store abstraction |
| `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | EF Core provider for Postgres |
| `pgvector` (Npgsql plugin) | pgvector support for embeddings |
| `UglyToad.PdfPig` | PDF text extraction |

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all required NuGet packages
- Add Blazor Server services and configuration to `Program.cs`
- Create `appsettings.json` with configuration sections (AI, ConnectionStrings)

### 2. Data models & EF Core context
- `Models/DocumentChunk.cs` вҖ” represents a chunk of PDF content with its embedding vector
- `Models/ChatSession.cs` вҖ” a user chat session
- `Models/ChatMessage.cs` вҖ” individual messages within a session
- `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` вҖ” EF Core DbContext for Postgres

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `Services/DocumentIngestionService.cs`
  - Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
  - Extract text using PdfPig
  - Split into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)
  - Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
  - Store chunks + embeddings in Postgres/pgvector
  - Skip already-ingested files (hash-based dedup)

### 4. Chat service (RAG pipeline)
- `Services/ChatService.cs`
  - Accept user query
  - Generate query embedding
  - Vector similarity search against document chunks (top-5, min score 0.75)
  - Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution
  - Stream LLM response via `IChatClient`
  - Persist messages to chat history

- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` вҖ” app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` вҖ” main chat interface
  - Message history display
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming response rendering
  - Source document references shown per response

- Wire up DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, DbContext, services
- Add health checks for Postgres and OpenAI
- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
- EF Core migrations for database schema

- Set temperature=0 for factual Q&A
- Set MaxOutputTokens to control cost
- Pin model version (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18)

- Set relevance threshold on vector search (0.75)
- Source attribution in responses
- API key from environment/user-secrets, never hardcoded
- Log token usage per request

# HelpChatbot вҖ” RAG-based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem

Build a web-based LLM chatbot using Blazor that helps customers get answers from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Technology Selection

Based on the .NET AI technology decision tree, this is a **RAG pipeline** (semantic search + LLM generation over unstructured text). The correct stack is:

| Layer | Technology | Why |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| **UI** | Blazor Server (.NET 10) | Interactive chat UI with server-side rendering, real-time streaming |
| **LLM abstraction** | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic chat interface; single promptвҶ’response with context injection (no agentic loops needed) |
| **LLM provider** | OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini) | Cost-effective for Q&A; swap later via MEAI abstraction |
| **Embeddings** | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` via `IEmbeddingGenerator` | Generates vectors for chunks and queries |
| **Vector store** | Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData + Npgsql/pgvector provider | Postgres-native vector similarity search |
| **Document ingestion** | PdfPig for PDF parsing + custom chunking | Extract text from PDFs, split into semantic chunks |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL with pgvector extension | Relational data (chat history) + vector search in one database |
| **ORM** | Entity Framework Core + Npgsql | Chat history, session management |

**Why not Microsoft.Agents.AI?** This is a straightforward RAG scenario (retrieve context вҶ’ augment prompt вҶ’ generate response). No tool calling, multi-step reasoning, or agentic loops are required. `IChatClient` is the correct layer.

- Add Blazor Server services and configuration to `Program.cs`
- Create `appsettings.json` with configuration sections (AI, ConnectionStrings)

- `Models/DocumentChunk.cs` вҖ” represents a chunk of PDF content with its embedding vector

- `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` вҖ” EF Core DbContext for Postgres

- `Services/DocumentIngestionService.cs`
  - Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files

+### 3. PDF ingestion service
+- `Services/DocumentIngestionService.cs`
+  - Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
+  - Extract text using PdfPig
+  - Split into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens)
+  - Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
+  - Store chunks + embeddings in Postgres/pgvector
+  - Skip already-ingested files (hash-based dedup)
+
+### 4. Chat service (RAG pipeline)
+- `Services/ChatService.cs`
+  - Accept user query
+  - Generate query embedding
+  - Vector similarity search against document chunks (top-5, min score 0.75)
+  - Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution
+  - Stream LLM response via `IChatClient`
+  - Persist messages to chat history
+
+### 5. Blazor UI
+- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` вҖ” app shell
+- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` вҖ” main chat interface
+  - Message history display
+  - Input box with send button
+  - Streaming response rendering
+  - Source document references shown per response
+- `wwwroot/css/app.css` вҖ” chat-specific styling
+
+### 6. Configuration & startup
+- Wire up DI: IChatClient, IEmbeddingGenerator, DbContext, services
+- Add health checks for Postgres and OpenAI
+- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
+- EF Core migrations for database schema
+
+### 7. Guardrails (from technology-selection skill)
+- Set temperature=0 for factual Q&A
+- Set MaxOutputTokens to control cost
+- Pin model version (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18)
+- Implement retry logic on LLM calls
+- Set relevance threshold on vector search (0.75)
+- Source attribution in responses
+- API key from environment/user-secrets, never hardcoded
+- Log token usage per request
+
+## Notes & Considerations
+
+- **pgvector extension**: Postgres must have the `vector` extension installed. The app will attempt `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector` during migration.
+- **Chunking strategy**: Paragraph-based semantic chunking with ~500 token windows and 50-token overlap for context continuity.
+- **Embedding dimensions**: `text-embedding-3-small` produces 1536-dimensional vectors.
+- **Streaming**: Use `GetStreamingResponseAsync` + `IAsyncEnumerable` for time-to-first-token optimization in the chat UI.
+- **File watching**: Initial version ingests on startup. Future enhancement: `FileSystemWatcher` for hot-reload when new PDFs are added.
+- **No authentication**: Initial version is internal-only, no auth. Can be added later.
+
